items.title: Ford says no one in Canada is scrutinized like he is after backtracking on $28.9M private jet purchase
Parody news site The Onion says it has a new plan to acquire Infowars.com
items.summary_auto: The parody news site The Onion says it has a new plan to acquire the conspiracy website Infowars.com, something it has been trying to do since the site first came up for sale two years ago.

items.title: U.S. singer D4vd charged with murder of teen girl found dead in his Tesla
items.summary_auto: The Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office said the 21-year-old D4vd, whose legal name is David Burke, was charged with first-degree murder in the killing of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who was reported missing by her family in 2024, when she was 13. Authorities say she was 14 when she died.

items.title: Kenya's Korir successfully defends Boston Marathon title; Canadian Linkletter posts personal-best time
items.summary_auto: Defending champion John Korir broke the Boston Marathon course record on Monday, riding a tailwind to a win in two hours one minute 42 seconds, the fifth-fastest marathon of all time. Calgary-born Rory Linkletter ran his best marathon in 2:06:04.

items.title: Man accused of killing RCMP officer in Burnaby, B.C., found not fit to stand trial
items.summary_auto: Jongwon Ham, accused of first-degree murder in the death of Burnaby RCMP Const. Shaelyn Yang, has been found not fit to stand trial in B.C. Supreme Court.

items.title: Man accused of killing RCMP officer in Burnaby, B.C. found not fit to stand trial
items.summary_auto: Jongwon Ham, accused of first-degree murder in the death of Burnaby RCMP Const. Shaelyn Yang, has been found not fit to stand trial in B.C. Supreme Court.

items.title: Feds announce 'incremental increase' in direct flights to and from China
items.summary_auto: Ottawa has announced an increase in the number of direct flights permitted to bring passengers and cargo to and from China.

items.title: Nike backtracks after 'walkers tolerated' Boston Marathon ad draws outrage
items.summary_auto: Runners accused Nike of being tone-deaf for a sign it posted in a store along the route for the Boston Marathon, which is also the premier marathon event for para athletes and adaptive runners.

items.title: U.K. prime minister says he didn't know Epstein-linked ambassador failed vetting process
items.summary_auto: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer spoke today about the fact that Peter Mandelson was appointed ambassador to the U.S. despite a recommendation against him receiving security clearance.

items.title: Electric ferry aims to reduce underwater noise, but threats still remain for humpbacks in B.C.
items.summary_auto: A new all-electric passenger ferry is moving ahead and while it will reduce underwater noise pollution, marine mammal experts and researchers says it is increasing another risk to humpback whales.

items.title: Oilers lean into their playoff maturity ahead of Game 1 against Ducks
items.summary_auto: The Edmonton Oilers climbed a playoff mountain the last two years, but didn't plant their flag on the summit.

items.title: How reality show MILF Manor lead to wholesome Canadian rescue show Must Love Dogs
items.summary_auto: A wholesome dog rescue show isn't the kind of project you'd expect to share DNA with one of reality TV's most unhinged dating experiments. And yet, that's exactly where the story behind CBC's new docuseries Must Love Dogs begins, says one of the leads.

items.title: Former fundamentalist minister in B.C. found not guilty of historical sexual abuse
items.summary_auto: Lee-Ann McChesney acquitted in B.C. Supreme Court in New Westminster on charges of sexual assault and sexual exploitation dating back to 1989.

items.title: Israel condemns soldier's desecration of crucifix in south Lebanon
items.summary_auto: Israel's prime minister, foreign minister and military on Monday condemned the desecration of a crucifix smashed by an Israeli soldier in a southern Lebanese village home to Christians.

items.title: Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.4% in March, driven by high gas prices
items.summary_auto: Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.4 per cent in March, Statistics Canada said on Monday. Economists expected the rising cost of fuel to weigh heavily on inflation, as the oil crunch in the Strait of Hormuz has driven gas prices up worldwide.

items.title: First Nation seeks federal funding to support first on-reserve birthing lodge in Sask.
items.summary_auto: A new birthing and childrearing lodge is nearly ready to open on Sturgeon Lake First Nation, but leaders say the bigger question is whether it can operate at full capacity.

items.title: Bulgaria's Kremlin-friendly ex-president set for landslide election win
items.summary_auto: A pro-Russian former president, Rumen Radev, is set for a runaway victory in Bulgaria's election and may even secure a parliamentary majority, exit polls showed, potentially ending years of weak coalition governments and altering the European Union member's foreign policy.

items.title: Is a global food crisis looming?
items.summary_auto: The U.S.-Israeli  war on Iran has sparked a global energy crisis. Is a food crisis next?

items.title: Japan issues advisory for slightly increased risk of mega-quake in northern coastal areas
items.summary_auto: Japan issued an advisory Monday for an increased risk of a possible mega-quake in northern coastal areas, induced by a major earthquake earlier in the day.

items.title: Fish feud: Atlantic salmon farmers, conservationists clash over promised federal funding
items.summary_auto: A salmon war is bubbling up in the waters of Atlantic Canada. On one side are fish farmers who raise salmon in ocean pens. On the other is a conservation group called the Atlantic Salmon Federation.

items.title: An earlier start to P.E.I.’s spring lobster season is proving challenging at some harbours
items.summary_auto: The spring lobster season gets underway on April 25 this year,  one week earlier than the traditional start date of May 1. That's causing problems for at least one of the Island's larger harbours. CBC’s Nancy Russell explains.

items.title: What can be done to reduce LED headlight glare?
items.summary_auto: A Transport Canada survey that asks drivers to weigh in on car headlight glare closes April 20, 2026. Experts and everyday drivers in Nova Scotia have some strong opinions to share.

items.title: Fish feud: Salmon farmers, conservationists clash over promised federal funding
items.summary_auto: A salmon war is bubbling up in the waters of Atlantic Canada. On one side are fish farmers who raise salmon in ocean pens. On the other is a conservation group called the Atlantic Salmon Federation.

items.title: Documents show Horizon staff raised alarm about ambulance bay unit long before public backlash
items.summary_auto: Horizon Health staff raised concerns ranging from substandard infection prevention to equipment and temperature controls.

items.title: Enterprise demanded almost $10K from couple over alleged diesel damage — evidence suggests otherwise
items.summary_auto: A B.C. couple says they returned a rental car without issue — then months later were told they owed almost $10K for diesel damage. Receipts, vehicle design and expert opinion contradict the claim, which Enterprise dropped after legal pressure and Go Public inquiries.

items.title: New film about Hamilton teen Devan Selvey, murdered at school, aims to help make all students safer
items.summary_auto: Over six years since her son Devan was murdered outside his Hamilton high school, Shari-Ann Selvey says not enough has changed to protect kids. To raise awareness about issues including bullying and youth crime, Selvey is working with Hamilton filmmaker Tyrone Greenidge on a documentary about Devan.

items.title: 'There's no guidance': Quebec daycares say new secularism law causing fear, uncertainty
items.summary_auto: Adopted earlier this month, Bill 9 bans workers in publicly funded daycares from wearing religious symbols on the job. Many daycare operators say they’re still in the dark about how the law is supposed to work.

items.title: Habs take Game 1 in Tampa with hat trick from Slafkovsky
items.summary_auto: Juraj Slafkovsky scored his third power-play goal 1:22 into overtime and the Montreal Canadiens beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 4-3 in Game 1 of their first-round series on Sunday night.

items.title: Jeremy Hansen's wife calls Artemis II re-entry 'the most terrifying day of my life'
items.summary_auto: In an exclusive Canadian interview with The National, Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen and his wife, Catherine, tell CBC's Ian Hanomansing about their respective experiences witnessing the history-making Artemis II mission from the far side of the moon and on the ground in mission control.
